evals.name: report-broken-fountain
evals.prompt: I want to let users report a fountain as broken or dry, and show that status to others. Help me plan it before we build.
evals.expected_output: Explores the codebase (community/Supabase, data flow) first, then asks structured AskUserQuestion screens covering the real forks (where status is stored, who can report, how it's surfaced, abuse/trust) — each with concrete options, tradeoffs, and a recommended answer. Ends with a concrete implementation plan reflecting the choices.

evals.name: grill-existing-plan
evals.prompt: Here's my plan: add a 'Nearby toilets' push notification when the user is walking and within 100m of a public toilet. Grill me on it before I start.
evals.expected_output: Pressure-tests the stated plan via structured AskUserQuestion screens on the genuine unknowns (battery/geofencing approach, notification frequency/throttling, permissions, opt-in). Concrete options with tradeoffs and a recommendation each. Closes with a refined plan.

evals.name: ambiguous-perf
evals.prompt: The map feels slow when there are lots of markers. I want to make it fast. Plan the work.
evals.expected_output: Investigates the map rendering code first (markers, clustering, sources), then asks structured questions resolving the ambiguity in 'fast' (which slowness, target, approach: clustering vs virtualization vs fewer features) with tradeoffs and a recommended pick. Produces a plan grounded in the actual code.
